compiled_state.title: Add Phase 2 confidence routing table and per-area inline rules to SKILL.md (d875-4466)
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
UPDATE

## Context
Implements the Phase 2 confidence routing in SKILL.md. Uses Proposal B approach: routing table at Phase 2 entry + per-area inline confidence blocks for all 7 areas + non-technical path defaults. Makes T1 RED tests pass GREEN.

## What to Build
In plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md, at the Phase 2 Socratic Dialogue Loop section (after Dialogue Rules, before Question Guide by Area):

### 1. Add Confidence Routing Table (after existing Dialogue Rules)

```
### Confidence Routing

Before asking each question, consult the CONFIDENCE_CONTEXT in $SCRATCHPAD:

| Confidence level | Action |
|-----------------|--------|
| **high** | Skip the question entirely. Show a one-line summary: "Detected [value] — skipping [area] question." |
| **medium** | Show pre-filled answer: "I detected [value]. Does this look right? [Y/n]" — accept confirmation or correction. |
| **low** | Ask normally using the Question Guide below. |

Missing dimensions default to **low** (ask normally).
```

### 2. Add Non-Technical Path section (after routing table)

```
### Non-Technical Path

When comfort_level is "non-technical", skip or default engineering-specific questions:
- commands dimension: use detected commands or default to `make test` / `make lint`; never ask about npm vs. Docker invocation style
- ci dimension: use detected CI workflow filename; skip deep CI trigger configuration questions
- enforcement dimension: apply recommended DSO defaults; skip technical gate configuration questions

For non-technical users, confirm detected values rather than asking open-ended engineering questions.
```

### 3. Per-area inline confidence blocks in each area section

For each of the 7 areas (stack, commands, architecture, infrastructure, ci, design, enforcement), add confidence routing before the existing question template:

Stack example:
```
**Confidence routing:**
- high: "Detected [stack] — skipping stack question."
- medium: "I detected [stack]. Does this look right? [Y/n]"
- low: Ask using templates below.
```

## Done Definitions Satisfied
- DD1: high-confidence skip with "Detected X — skipping Y question" summary
- DD2: non-technical user never sees engineering-specific questions
- DD3: medium-confidence shows "Does this look right? [Y/n]"
- DD4: routing reads CONFIDENCE_CONTEXT which already includes Phase 0.5 elevations
- DD5: T1 tests pass GREEN

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SKILL.md Phase 2 contains a Confidence Routing table with all 3 tiers
  Verify: grep -qE 'high.*[Ss]kip|medium.*[Cc]onfirm|low.*[Aa]sk' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md
- [ ] SKILL.md Phase 2 contains "Does this look right?" confirm template
  Verify: grep -q 'Does this look right' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md
- [ ] SKILL.md Phase 2 contains non-technical path section
  Verify: grep -qi 'Non-Technical Path\|non-technical' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md
- [ ] SKILL.md Phase 2 contains skip summary pattern ("Detected" + "skipping")
  Verify: grep -q 'skipping' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md
- [ ] All T1 tests pass GREEN
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/skills/test-onboarding-skill.sh 2>&1 | grep -v FAIL
- [ ] RED marker [test_phase2_confidence_routing_table_present] removed from .test-index (tests GREEN)
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/hooks/record-test-status.sh --restart --source-file plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md
